programmes.show_description: Solana Group CEO Crystal Hennessy-Vass sweeps into the resort and takes Joyce out to lunch to announce that the staff budget is being cut by half - and Joyce is being replaced. S7 E5
When he arrives home to find a nine-year-old boy on his doorstep, Terry takes on a minding job he hadn't anticipated and later teaches someone a lesson. S2 E5: Not a Bad Lad, Dad
The story of bad tempered Alf Garnett who has not mellowed with the years and is as bigoted as ever. S1 E1
Jo is going on a date with Philip but has forgotten that she is meant to be babysitting; Chrissy agrees to take her place and persuades Robin to come with her. S2 E5: Two Foot Two, Eyes Of Blue
Returning from a boat trip to Tangier, Amy is approached by a Spaniard who asks her to deliver a box allegedly containing the ashes of his dead friend Pepe to his widowed mother. S2 E5: Pepe

The landscape of Southern England has many rural retreats that have been served by railway lines with enduring links to their local communities.

Mrs Boswell bonds with new neighbour Celia, and Billy manages to upset Adrian in the middle of a poetry recital in the kitchen. S5 E4
Harold picks up a rare antique figurine on his rounds bearing the well-known crossed swords; thinking he's found an item worth selling, he is determined to make a large profit. S4 E2: Crossed Swords
It's Jack and May's wedding anniversary, but May is worried that she might be pregnant - meanwhile, Arnold fears that he is about to get the sack. S2 E9: A Pregnant Pause
Rigsby decides to move on, give up on Miss Jones and file for divorce - he places an advert in the local newspaper, but his search for love doesn't run entirely smoothly. S4 E4: Pink Carnations
When the lease is up on the flat and George wants to increase the rent, the flatmates decide that the only way to raise the money is to get a fourth tenant to share. S6 E2: One More for the Pot
Ron unexpectedly turns up at the Sparrow household with suitcase in hand. Phoebe suggests Gary should assume teaching duties for the local children. S3 E6: Goodnight Children Everywhere

Sitcom starring Michael Crawford. The new manager of a labour exchange refuses to believe that Frank is unemployable. S1 E7: The Employment Exchange

Rigsby is ecstatic to learn that he has inherited his Uncle George's fortune but distressed at a clause that he must be happily married. S4 E3: Great Expectations
Terry helps Bob move his most treasured possessions from his old house to the new one, but Thelma refuses to have his junk in their home. S1 E9: Storm in a Tea Chest

Alf's eyesight is worsening, which is fine until he accidentally enters the Ladies' toilet and is arrested as a sex pest. S1 E2
Robin has failed his cookery exam and considers moving back to Southampton, but the girls are far from pleased at the thought of his mate Larry moving in. S2 E6: Carry Me Back to Old Southampton

Arthur gets involved with Clive Cosgrove, who has several businesses that are each run by a woman he is married to. S5 E3: A Number of Old Wives' Tales
Elizabeth and her daughter return to Eaton Place, but Sarah dislikes Nanny Webster as she is very set in her ways and seems not to be able to cope as she used to. S2 E8: Out of the Everywhere
Mrs Boswell has a bit of trouble with her brood. Billy returns to his estranged wife Julie, while Adrian reaches a plateau. S5 E5
Harold thinks the family home needs central heating; Albert is delighted until he hears that his son plans to install it all himself. S4 E3: Those Magnificent Men and Their Heating Machines
The staff discover that the station is a hundred years old and decide to have a street party to celebrate. S2 E10: Ton Up
New resident Ambrose claims he has the power of hypnosis due to his gypsy blood - Rigsby is sceptical but soon wonders if Ambrose's abilities could be used in his favour. S4 E5: Under The Influence
When Mildred is going to the Floral Society annual dinner dance but George doesn't want to, Robin agrees to accompany Mildred to the dance and leaves George rather jealous. S6 E3: The Generation Game
Gary bumps into his former talent agent, Sidney Wix, which leads to an unwelcome collaboration with superstar ukulele player, George Formby. S3 E7: Turned Out Nice Again

Classic sitcom with one-man disaster area Frank Spencer. Frank and Betty go for a spin in his new car and end up having a clifftop picnic. S2 E1: Cliffhanger
Dorien's book group is convening to discuss Crime and Punishment, which turns out to be rather applicable to the events that unfold in Tracey's household. S11 E5: Tracey's Choice

Terry is disappointed that Bob has asked someone else to be his best man - after a few drinks at the restaurant, they start to chat up a couple of pretty girls. S1 E10: The Old Magic
Arthur enters the food business, but when the chef he employs goes on the run from a group of gangsters, Arthur is left to cater for a policeman's retirement party. S8 E10: Too Many Crooks
Mateo's mother-in-law is threatening to kill him. Tiger and Terri come up with a plan to get Clive's 5,000 euros back from Monty. Riannon arrives - and there is no sign of Donald. S7 E7
